California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
April 22, 1991
Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
City Attorney
city of Carlsbad
12 00 carlsbad village Drive
carlsbad. cA 92008-1989
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-91-054
Dear Mr. Biondo:
You have requested advice concerning application of the
con f l ict-of- i nterest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act'.)1 to the duties of carlsbad city councilmember Margaret
Stanton. she is also a nernber of the CarLsbad Redevelopment Con-
nission. The following advice is based upon the facts provided in
your Ietter and our telePhone conversation on March 22, L99L.
OUESTION
Under the Act, may ljts. Stanton participate in certain
projects pending before the redeveloPnent commission?
CONCLUS TON
Ms. Stanton may participate in projects pending before the
redevelopnent cornnission if there wiIl. not be a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on either Jazzercise, Inc.
or on her real property interest.
FACTS
Ms. Stanton is an off j.cer and director of Jazzercise, fnc.
She is the general manager of the cornpany and an ernployee of the
corporation. She does not have any ownership interest j.n the
1 co\r"..rr"nt code Sections 81OOo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the covernment Code unl,ess othererise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428J Street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8oz . sacramento cA 95go4.o8oz . (916)322-5660


File No. A-9■
-054
Page 2
corporation, which is closely-held and wholly-owned by the Missett
Family Trust.
Jazzercise operates approxinately 4,500 franchises worldwide.
rts revenues from the franchises, a maif order and retail store
and its television operation are approxinateLy S15 million annu-
ally. 
You stated that the pretax incorne of Jazzercise is ap-
proximately 91 nillion, 
the net incorne exceeds 94oo,000 and the
tangible assets are approxirnately $2 mil1ion.
The corporate office is located at 2808 Roosevelt Street,
which is in the redevelopment area of carlsbad. The corporation
signed a five year Iease for this office in october 1990. The
corporation operates a nail order and retail store and a televi-
sion production company at this Iocation. It also subleases a
part of the offices to severaL of its local franchisees for dance
fitness classes. These franchisees pay Jazzercise 35 percent of
the gross student fees collected per month as rent and 20 percent
of the gross student fees coLlected as a continuing franchise fee.
The city of carlsbad adopted a redevelopment plan in 1981. It
includes six subareas with specific goals and desired land uses'
The Jazzercise corporate offices are located within subarea 5,
which is planned al the rnajor tourist and related comrnercial
center for the project area. The following redevelopment proiects
are pending and will go to the redevelopment commission for a vote
in the near future:
1. A parking pIan, including financing sources, which
identifies how parking needs for the village redevelopment area
may be met for iuture projects. 
Preparation of the parking plan
wai funded by the redevelopnent tax increment and benefits the
entire proje-t area. Implementation rnay involve the imposiEion of
additional fees on property ovners Iocated within the area to
finance the necessary improvernents. It is also possible a
surcharge will be added to the business license tax-
2. The redeve.Lopment comnj.ssion is completing a major
streetscape project funded by tax alLocation bonds. A fifth 
phase
of this project consisting of street irnprovenents, decorative pav-
ers, storm drains, traffic signals, street furniture, art work and
street lighting is ready for approval. Phase II wj'I1 cost ap-
proximately 51.2 million. 
It tiII 
be located on Carlsbad ViIIage
Drive (ELm Avenue) fro:n washington street to Pio Pico. Traffic
signals will be installed along crand Avenue. The project is
Iocated 5oo to l,OoO feet from the Jazzercise corporate offices.
3. The final design of a rapid rail transit center is beinq
proposed and funded by the county of San Diego and the North
county Transit District, a separate legal entity. 
The redevelop-
ment comrnission has signed a cooperative agreenent with both agen-
cj.es which requires the city's approval of the f inal. design. The
project will include a transit center structure, parking
facilities 
and lighting. 
A bus transfer facility 
west of the


File No. A-9■
-054
Page 3
railroad tracks will be constructed requiring relocation of an
existing lumber business on state street. 
The rapid rail transit
center station site will- be bounded on three sides by carlsbad
viltage Drive (ELm Avenue), state street and Grand Avenue. It is
approximately 1,OOO feet frorn the Jazzercise office.
4. The redevelopment commission staff is preparing a ltlaster
plan to determine ho!, the redevelopment area will develop in the
next ten to tlrenty years. ELenents of the plan will include, but
are not Iinited to, the design, land use, historic preservation
and cultural arts, and financing of projects in the redevelopment
area. The pl-an will be funded frorn the redevelopment tax incre-
ment.
5. The city council has authorized the study and possible
formation of a second redevelopment project area generally located
along the Pacific ocean and to the south of the existing
redevelopment area. The closest point of the proposed second
redevelopment area is 2.oOO feet from the Jazzercise corporate
offices. 
The outer boundary is 6.5 miles away. It is also
located approximately 2.oOO feet from Ms. Stanton's personal
residence. The next action wiII be appointment of the project
area conmittee and designation of the new project area.
6. The design review board within the redevelopment agency
nakes recornrnendat ions to the agency on specific projects. 
This
board has recommended approval of a Sl mi11j.on private development
project which would allor.r the construction of a three story,
1e,afi square foot rnixed-use office and retail building, Iocated
at the southeast corner of State Street and Laguna Drive ap-
proximately 2,000 feet from the Jazzercise corporate offices.
7. An application has been submitted for a 3,100 square foot
retail project, cos'uing S15o,0oo and Iocated at 2933 Roosevelt
street. 250 feet from the Jazzercise corporate offices.
8. Another application is pending for a S25,000 project at
2558 Roosevelt street, 5oo feet from the Jazzercise offices. 
It
consists of an intensification of the existing general office use
on the site to nedical use and requires parking provisions on an
enjoining Iot by private lease.
9. within the redevelopnent area there is an Army/Navy
acaderny located at the northlrest corner of cyPress Avenue and
CarLsbad Boulevard. This nonprofit organization j.s preparing for
a totaL renovation of its campus and has proposed a S20 million
construction project, whj.ch is located 1,000 feet fron the
J azzerci.se corporate offices.
10. A 52.5 rnillion senior housing project is proposed at the
southwest corner of Carlsbad Boulevard and Beech Avenue, r.rhich is
Iocated fron 1,000 to 2,000 feet from the Jazzercise offices.


File No. A-91-054
Page 4
11. A nixed-use retail and a condoniniun apartnent complex
vrith 20 housing units is proposed for the southeast corner of
Carlsbad Boulevard and Beech Avenue, 750 to 2,000 feet frorn the
Jazzercise offices. 
The project will cost approximately S15.5
nillion and nay require financial assistance from the redeveLop-
ment agency.
ANALYS T S
The Act prohibits a public official 
frorn rnaking, participat-
ing in, or using her official 
position to influence a governmental
decision in which she knows or has reason to kno$, she has a
financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financiaL effect,
distinguishable frorn its effect on the public Aenera}ly, on the
official 
or a member of her i:nmediate family or on, anong other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty doI-
Iars ($250) or nore in value provided to, received
by or pronised to the public official 
within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of nanagement.
(e) Any donor of, or any inter:nediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars (5250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official 
within 12 months prior to the tine when
the decision is made -
Section 87103(a)―
(e).
As a public official, 
Ms.
governnental decision if it is
decision r+iI1 have a material
income of over S25O within t2
Stanton rnay not participate in a
reasonably foreseeable that the
financial effect on any source of
months prior to the decision, on a


File No. A-9■
-054
Page 5
business entity in which she is an officer or director, or on any
real property interest. 
(Section 871O3 (a) , (b), and (e) . )
Foreseeabilitv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial lj.kelihood that they will occur. To be foresee-
able, the effects of a decision must be more than a mere possibil-
ity; however, certainty is not required. 
(Downev cares v. Dovrnev
Communitv Development Com. (1987) 196 caL.App.3d 983t 9a9-991i
witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 CaI.App.3d 817, 822i In re Thorner (1975)
l FPPC Ops. 198 (copy enclosed). ) The Act seeks to prevent nore
than actual conflicts of interest, it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. 
(witt v. Morrow,
supra, at 823 . )
The purpose of any redevelopment plan is to promote sound
development and redevelopment of blighted areas. The anticipated
result of redevelopment is an increase in property values and an
improved business climate within the project area, which benefits
the community as a whole. The very nature of redevelopment
projects has led the commission to find that it is reasonably
foreseeable that there wiII be a financial effect on real property
val.ues and business interests located within or near project
areas. (In re oqlesbv (1975) I FPPC ops. 71, Advice Letters to
Haiqht, No. A-81-509, and PhiIIips, No. A-87-165, ccpies enclosed.
See also Downev cares v. Downev comnunitv DeveloDnent com., suDra.
In addition, the Iocation of the various redeve.l'opment
projects withj.n a short distance of the Jazzercise corporate of-
fices, where part of the office space is leased to local
franchises for dance fitness classes, may also have a foreseeable
financial effect on ihe Jazzercise business. (See fn re Gillnor
(L9'77) 3 FPPC Ops. 38, bottom of page 41, copy enclosed. )
Materia I itv
Regulation 18702 sets forth the guj.deli.nes for determining
whether an official's 
econonic interest in a decision is 'rmateri-
aIly" affected as required by section 87103. If the officia]'s
economic interest is directlv involved in the decision, Regulation
L8702.:, applies to deterrnine nateriaLity. 
However, if the
official's 
econonic interest is indirectl-v affected by the deci-
sion, Regulations L8702.2 lo L8'702.6 apply to determine whether
the effect of the decision is rnaterial.
Ms. Stanton is an officer, director, general nanager and
employee of Jazzercise, Inc. Jazzercise nay be indirectly af-
fected by certain decisions regarding future projects in or near
the redevelopnent area. Regulation L87O2.2 (copy enclosed)
provides guidelines to ascertain whether the effect of a decision
on a business entity indirectly involved in a decision is mate-
rial. 
You have stated that the financial standards in ReguJ.ation


Fil-e No. A-91-o54
Page 6
tA702.2(f ) apply to Jazzercise. Under this regulation, the effect
of a decision is material as to a business entity in which the
official- has an econornic interest if:
(f) 
For any business entity not covered by
subdivisions (a) or (b) thich meets the financial
standards for listing on the National Association
of Securities Dealers National Market List, the
tests in subdivision (c) nay be applied. The
standards are as follows: 
The business entity has
net tangible assets of at least 54,000,0o0, and had
pre-tax incone for the last fiscal year of at least
$z:o,ooo, with net income frorn that perj-od of at
least $400,000.
The tests in subdivision (c) are:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $30,000 or more,' or
(2) The decision will- result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additionaL expenses or
reducing or elirninating existing expenses for a
f j.scaL year in the amount of S'7,500 or more; or
(3) The decision wiII result in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of S30,000 or more.
Therefore, you nust apply the tests in subdivision (c) to
each redevelopment project and decision enumerated above to
deterrnine if Lhere wifl ne a material financial effect on
Jazzercise. lf Jazzercise is naterially affected, !,1s. stanton may
not partj,cipate in either the cj.ty council or redevelopment con-
mission decisions.
Project nunber 5 discusses the possible formation of a second
redevelopment project which is approximately 2,0o0.feet fron Ms'
Stanton's personal residence. Pursuant to Regulation
:18702.3 (a) (3) (A) (copy enclosed) . if the decision wi}l have a
reasonably foreseeable financial effect of S10,0O0 or more on the
fair narket value of lts. Stanton's property, she may not
participate in the decision.
Public cenera L Iv
Even if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a
decision is rnaterial, disqua I if ication is required only if the
effect if distinguishable fron the effect on the public generally.
(Section 87103.) For the city council's jurisdiction, 
the public
ccnsists of aII residents of the city. 
Thus, d isqua 1i fication is
required unless the redevel.opnent decisions wiII affect Ms-


File No. A-91-054
Page 7
stanton's economic interests in substantially the sane nanner as
they lriLl affect all residents of the city, or a significant seg-
menl of the residents of the city. 
Since the Jazzetcise corporate
office is located within the redevefopnent area, it is highly
unJ.ikely that Jazzercise will be affected in the saltre manner as
the public generally. 
(See In re Leqan (1985) 9 FPPC ops. 1, coPy
enclosed. )
Ms. stanton wilt also have to examine the effect of the
second redevelopment project on her personal residence to
ascertain if she wiII be affected in a nanner distinguishable frorn
the public generally.
I trust this answers your question. If you have any further
questions regarding this natter, please contact me at (916)
322-590L.
s incere Iy ,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel
By:
